sourceMovie.title: Scent of a Woman
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Scent of a Woman for the same reason it broke through in 1992: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Martin Brest keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Forrest Gump
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Scent of a Woman clicked for you, Forrest Gump (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Scent of a Woman clicked for you, Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Scent of a Woman?
faq.answer: Try Forrest Gump, Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind, Léon: The Professional—each shares DNA with Scent of a Woman while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Scent of a Woman?
faq.answer: Scent of a Woman (1992) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Scent of a Woman worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
